# ============================================================================
# Set 2 perception config - white cubes with fruit images on 3 of 6 faces, in
# the same 4x4 m arena. SEPARATE from Set 1 (own dataset, models, thresholds,
# runtime policy). Every tunable for the Isaac Sim data generation lives here so
# the sim files stay generic. Edit values to match the real cubes / arena.
# ============================================================================

set: set2

classes:
  fruits: [apple, orange, banana, pineapple]   # classifier fruit classes
  detector: [cube_candidate]                   # detector: single class
  unknown: unknown                             # classifier reject class

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- assets
# Fruit images used as randomized printed/sticker labels. Drop multiple real
# photos per class into these folders (png/jpg). sim/make_fruit_textures.py can
# generate distinguishable PLACEHOLDERS so the pipeline runs before real images
# arrive - replace them with real fruit photos for sim->real quality.
assets:
  fruit_texture_dir: assets/fruit_textures   # <dir>/<fruit>/*.png|jpg
  # Optional: a cube body USD (e.g. converted from a real cube STL). If null/missing
  # the generator builds a clean procedural box (preferred for per-face texturing).
  cube_usd: null

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- arena (shared shape with set1)
arena:
  size_x: 4.0
  size_y: 4.0
  wall_height: 0.5
  wall_thickness: 0.05
  floor_color: [0.45, 0.30, 0.17]
  floor_color_jitter: 0.08
  floor_roughness: [0.5, 0.9]
  wall_color: [0.92, 0.92, 0.92]
  wall_color_jitter: 0.06
  wall_roughness: [0.4, 0.8]
  spawn_margin: 0.35

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- camera (NUROUM V11) - identical rig to set1
camera:
  width: 1280
  height: 720
  hfov_deg: 90.0
  focal_length: 10.4775
  horizontal_aperture: 20.955
  fx: 640.0
  fy: 640.0
  cx: 640.0
  cy: 360.0

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- robot sensor rig (identical to set1)
robot:
  base_height: 0.0
  cam_height: 0.20
  cam_forward_offset: 0.10
  cam_lateral_offset: 0.15
  cam_pitch_deg: -20.0
  cam_yaw_outward_deg: 30.0
  lidar_height: 0.30
  occlusion_proxy: false
  jitter:
    height: [-0.01, 0.01]
    pitch_deg: [-7.0, 7.0]     # -> effective pitch -13..-27 deg
    yaw_deg: [-3.0, 3.0]
    roll_deg: [-2.0, 2.0]

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- cubes / placement
cubes:
  size_m: 0.06                 # real cube edge length (~6 cm) - EDIT to match the real object
  size_jitter_m: 0.005         # per-frame edge variation
  count_range: [1, 4]          # cubes present per frame
  n_plain: 3                   # plain (no-fruit) white cubes in the pool -> 'unknown' crops
  z_on_floor: true
  fixed_face_layout: false     # true -> fruit always on the same 3 faces (real object);
                               # false -> randomize which 3 faces per cube (robustness)
  fruit_faces: 3               # exactly 3 of 6 faces carry fruit
  # White 3D-printed plastic body (UsdPreviewSurface).
  body_material:
    base_color: [0.88, 0.88, 0.88]
    color_jitter: 0.06
    roughness: [0.35, 0.7]
    metallic: 0.0
  # Fraction of cubes that are DISTRACTORS (no fruit at all -> always 'unknown'):
  #   plain white cubes + (if Set 1 USDs present) non-cube polyhedra.
  distractor_frac: 0.25
  set1_usd_dir: isaac/assets/usd      # reuse octa/dodeca/icosa as non-cube negatives if present
  neg_size_m: 0.08                    # rendered size of non-cube negatives
  neg_calib_m: 64.9                   # set1 render_calib quirk: rep scale 1.0 renders USD at ~64.9 m

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- fruit label (printed/sticker) realism
# A fruit image is rendered as a thin textured decal sitting flush on a face,
# simulating a printed/sticker label rather than a clean digital texture.
fruit_label:
  scale_frac: [0.55, 0.92]     # label size as fraction of the face edge
  offset_frac: 0.12            # random center offset on the face (+-frac of edge)
  rotation_deg: [-8, 8]        # in-plane misalignment
  border_frac: [0.0, 0.10]     # paper/sticker white border (fraction of label)
  raise_eps_m: 0.0008          # lift above the face to avoid z-fighting
  # Printed-look colour/exposure jitter applied via the texture scale/bias inputs:
  brightness: [0.8, 1.15]
  contrast: [0.85, 1.15]
  saturation: [0.8, 1.15]
  glare_prob: 0.25             # chance of a brighter specular label material
  roughness: [0.3, 0.7]

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- lighting / DR (shared with set1)
lighting:
  sun_intensity: [2000, 9000]
  sun_pitch_deg: [-70, -20]
  sun_yaw_deg: [-180, 180]
  dome_intensity: [600, 2500]
  color_temp: [4000, 8000]

postprocess:
  motion_blur: true
  gaussian_noise: true
  exposure_jitter: true

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- dataset output
dataset:
  root: datasets/set2
  detector_subdir: detector
  classifier_subdir: classifier
  metadata_subdir: metadata
  val_ratio: 0.15
  min_frame_brightness: 12

# Rules that turn a detected cube into a classifier sample vs `unknown`. The label
# is based on VISIBLE FRUIT EVIDENCE (analytic face-facing + projected fruit area),
# never on the cube's hidden physical identity.
labeling:
  detector_min_box_px: 8          # detector keeps boxes down to here (high recall)
  min_box_px: 36                  # min(w,h) px for a crop to be eligible for a fruit label
  min_visible_frac: 0.55          # 1 - occlusionRatio of the cube box
  max_truncation_px: 2            # cube box this close to the image border = truncated
  # Fruit-evidence gates (ALL must hold for a fruit-class label, else -> unknown):
  min_fruit_face_facing: 0.30     # max over fruit faces of (-normal . view_dir), 0..1
  min_fruit_area_ratio: 0.10      # visible fruit pixel area / cube box pixel area
  min_fruit_box_px: 14            # min(w,h) px of the visible fruit region
  # Classifier crop augmentation (simulate detector imperfection):
  crop_margin_frac: [-0.08, 0.20]
  crop_shift_frac: 0.10
  crops_per_object: 2
  # Hard-but-identifiable share: keep some borderline-but-clear fruit views as the
  # fruit class (not unknown) so the classifier still recognizes harder real views.
  hard_positive_keep: 0.5
  # Extra explicit unknown sources:
  background_unknown_frac: 0.3    # frames that also emit a background crop
  whiteface_unknown_boost: true   # white-face-only cube views -> unknown (the core case)

render:
  rt_subframes: 32

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- runtime (Jetson) - conservative, false-positive averse
runtime:
  detector_conf: 0.25
  detector_imgsz: 640
  # Only classify when the cube is close/large enough for reliable fruit reading:
  min_bbox_px: 48               # min(w,h) px (larger than set1: fruit detail needs resolution)
  min_bbox_area_ratio: 0.006
  reject_truncation_px: 3
  classifier_imgsz: 128
  # Conservative fruit acceptance (wrong pickup = -40, miss = 0):
  conf_threshold: 0.75          # calibrated softmax prob for top-1 fruit
  margin_threshold: 0.15        # top1 - top2 probability
  unknown_conf_relax: 0.50      # below this top-1 conf, treat as unknown regardless of class
  # Tracking + voting:
  track_iou: 0.3
  track_max_age: 12
  vote_window: 9
  min_confirmations: 3          # target-fruit votes needed for TARGET_CONFIRMED
  max_reobserve: 3              # active viewpoint changes for an unknown/uncertain cube
  unknown_patience: 4           # consecutive unknowns before requesting a viewpoint change
  # PICKUP_READY gating:
  pickup_min_bbox_px: 110       # cube must be close (large) to pick
  reconfirm_within: 3           # re-seen as target within N recent frames
  conflict_reject: 2            # >=N strong non-target-fruit votes -> REJECT (never pick)

lines: -  size_m: 0.06                 # real cube edge length (~6 cm) - EDIT to match the real object
+  size_m: 0.08                 # real cube edge length (8 cm)
n_plain: 3                   # plain (no-fruit) white cubes in the pool -> 'unknown' crops

> Assumptions to confirm against the real objects (edit `configs/set2.yaml`): cube edge
> `cubes.size_m` (default 0.06 m), and whether the fruit faces are a **fixed** layout
> (`cubes.fixed_face_layout: true` to match the real cubes exactly) or vary. Drop the real
> cube STL in as `assets.cube_usd` if you prefer it over the procedural box. Robot-body
> occlusion proxies are off by default (`robot.occlusion_proxy: false`) as in Set 1.
input.new_string:
> **Cube body**: the generator builds a clean procedural box at `cubes.size_m` (0.08 m).
> A real cube STL is **not needed** — a fruit cube is geometrically just a box, and the
> procedural body is what the decal placement + analytic projection math assume (a unit
> cube). Leave `assets.cube_usd: null`. (Converting your cube STL would normalize it to a
> different size and break that math, for no visual gain.)
>
> **Fruit images** (`assets/fruit_textures/<fruit>/*.png|jpg`): each image should look like
> the label as the camera will see it on the cube — the fruit filling a roughly **square**,
> **opaque** frame (transparency is ignored by the renderer, so flatten onto the real sticker
> background). Several variants per class improve robustness. avif/webp are not readable by the
> USD texture loader — convert to png/jpg.
>
> **Fruit-face layout** (`cubes.fixed_face_layout`): leave it `false`. The classifier reads
> *visible pixels*, never which physical face the fruit is on, and the cube is tumbled through
> all orientations each frame — so randomizing the 3 faces just adds viewpoint diversity. There
> is no need to match the real cube's exact face arrangement. Robot-body occlusion proxies are
> off by default (`robot.occlusion_proxy: false`), as in Set 1.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Set 2 — fruit-cube perception (Jetson Orin Nano)

Two-stage, conservative pipeline for competition Set 2: find white cubes from afar,
read the **visible fruit** on a cube only when close and the fruit is actually in view,
and pick only the announced target fruit. Wrong pickup = **−40**, miss = **0**, so the
whole system is tuned to **avoid false positives**, not to maximize recall.

**Set 1 and Set 2 are fully separate** (datasets, models, configs, thresholds, runtime
policy). Set 2 reuses only the shared, Set-agnostic infrastructure: `sim/arena_builder.py`,
`sim/robot_sensor_rig.py`, `sim/domain_randomization.py`, `runtime/tracking.py`,
`runtime/logging.py`, and the ONNX/TensorRT deployment utilities.

```
detector (1 class 'cube_candidate', high recall — fires on cubes with or without visible fruit)
        └─ crop ─► classifier (apple/orange/banana/pineapple/unknown, calibrated, conservative)
                          └─ per-camera tracker ─► Set2DecisionPolicy
                                 (UNKNOWN_CUBE→re-observe · TARGET_CONFIRMED→PICKUP_READY · REJECTED)
```

### The core principle
A cube shows fruit on **only 3 of 6 faces**, so a viewpoint may see only plain white. The
classifier must **never guess the fruit from a hidden face**:

| what the crop shows | label |
|---|---|
| a clearly visible, identifiable fruit image | that fruit class |
| plain white face only | `unknown` |
| fruit too small / blurry / occluded / edge-of-crop | `unknown` |
| Set 1 cube, non-cube object, background, detector false positive | `unknown` |

## Project layout (Set 2 files)
```
configs/   set2.yaml (all sim+runtime params), set2_detector.yaml (YOLO data), set2_classes.py
assets/    fruit_textures/{apple,orange,banana,pineapple}/*.png   (real photos; placeholders provided)
sim/       fruit_cube.py  make_fruit_textures.py  generate_set2_data.py     (+ shared arena/rig/DR)
training/  train_set2_detector.py  train_set2_classifier.py
runtime/   set2_pipeline.py  set2_decision_policy.py                        (+ shared tracking/logging)
deployment/ run_perception.py --set set2  export_set2_onnx.py  build_set2_tensorrt.py
models/set2/ detector/best.pt  classifier/{best.pt,classes.json,temperature.json,confusion.txt}
datasets/set2/ detector/{images,labels}/{train,val}  classifier/{train,val}/<class>  metadata/
```

## 1. Generate synthetic data (Isaac Sim, Windows)

The cube is a clean white box; each fruit image is laid on a face as a thin **textured
decal** — a faithful model of a printed/sticker label, and it makes white-face-only
views (the key `unknown` case) fall out of the camera geometry automatically. Exactly
**3 of 6 faces** carry fruit. Whether a crop is labelled `fruit` vs `unknown` is decided
**analytically** (`sim/fruit_cube.fruit_visibility`): which fruit faces actually point at
the camera and how large their projected area is — so the model learns *visible-fruit
recognition*, never hidden-cube guessing.

```powershell
robocopy \\wsl.localhost\Ubuntu-22.04\home\user\joon C:\joon /E /XD yolo runs datasets
$ISAAC = "C:\Users\user\Documents\IsaacSim\python.bat"
cd C:\joon
& $ISAAC sim\generate_set2_data.py --frames 9000          # -> datasets/set2/...
robocopy C:\joon\datasets\set2 \\wsl.localhost\Ubuntu-22.04\home\user\joon\datasets\set2 /E
```

Before the first run, put real fruit photos in `assets/fruit_textures/<fruit>/`, or generate
distinguishable placeholders so the pipeline runs end-to-end:
```bash
yolo/bin/python sim/make_fruit_textures.py        # 6 placeholders per class (REPLACE with real photos)
```

**Rendered viewpoints** are the real **NUROUM V11** side cameras (1280×720, 90° HFOV) at the
two robot mounts (~0.20 m, −20° pitch, forward-outward ±30°, with mount-error jitter — all in
`configs/set2.yaml → robot`). The generator alternates `left_camera`/`right_camera` and orbits
the cube cluster from near (classify) to far (detect-only). Randomized per frame: which 3 faces
get fruit, which fruit image, label scale/offset/rotation/border, printed-look
brightness/contrast/saturation + glare (via the texture `scale`/`bias` inputs), cube pose/size,
lighting, and the camera mount perturbation. Optional non-cube polyhedra (Set 1 USDs, if present
under `isaac/assets/usd/`) appear as **unlabelled hard negatives** and feed `unknown` crops.

**Outputs:** detector full-scene images + YOLO labels (class `0 cube_candidate`); classifier
crops under `<class>/` (fruit + a deliberately large `unknown` set, with crop margin/shift jitter
that simulates detector slop); per-frame `metadata/*.json` (camera, robot/cube poses, fruit class,
visible-fruit facing + area ratio, label/reason).

## 2. Train (WSL `yolo/` venv)
```bash
yolo/bin/python training/train_set2_detector.py   --epochs 120 --batch 32  # -> models/set2/detector/best.pt
yolo/bin/python training/train_set2_classifier.py --epochs 70  --imgsz 128 # -> models/set2/classifier/
```
- **Detector**: YOLO11n, single class `cube_candidate`, recall-leaning (low val conf, strong
  aug). It localizes cubes **with or without visible fruit**; non-cube polyhedra are background.
- **Classifier**: MobileNetV3-Small, 5 classes. Class-balanced sampler (`unknown` is large),
  heavy crop + perspective augmentation, **temperature scaling** for trustworthy confidence.
  `confusion.txt` reports the penalty-relevant metrics at the runtime gate: **unknown-leak
  rate** (junk accepted as a fruit → drives −40s, push toward 0), **fruit↔fruit confusion**,
  and **identifiable-fruit recall**.

## 3. Run onboard
```bash
# single camera dev test
yolo/bin/python deployment/run_perception.py --set set2 --target banana --source 0 --show --log
# both robot cameras
yolo/bin/python deployment/run_perception.py --set set2 --target banana --left 0 --right 1 --show
```
Flow per frame: detect → keep boxes that are **close/large/untruncated** (`min_bbox_px` 48,
`min_bbox_area_ratio`, `reject_truncation_px`) → crop → classify (calibrated) → IoU-track
**per camera** → vote over a window → policy. A cube that is `unknown` in one camera can be
identified by the other.

### Decision policy (conservative) — `runtime/set2_decision_policy.py`
A vote counts as **target/other-fruit** only if it is calibrated-confident *and* well-separated
(`conf_threshold` 0.75, `margin_threshold` 0.15); a weak fruit call (`conf < unknown_conf_relax`
0.50) is demoted to `unknown` so a hesitant guess never becomes evidence.
- `PICKUP_READY`: ≥`min_confirmations` (3) strong **target** votes, high avg conf, **no** strong
  other-fruit vote, more target than unknown, **and** the cube is close (`pickup_min_bbox_px` 110)
  and re-confirmed within the last `reconfirm_within` frames.
- `TARGET_CONFIRMED`: confirmed but not yet close → keep approaching.
- `TARGET_CANDIDATE` / `NON_TARGET_FRUIT`: partial evidence either way.
- `REJECTED`: ≥`conflict_reject` (2) strong **other-fruit** votes → skip permanently (never risk −40).
- `UNKNOWN_CUBE`: only unknowns. After `unknown_patience` (4) consecutive unknowns and while
  `reobserve_count < max_reobserve` (3) it sets `request_reobserve` — the navigator moves to a new
  viewpoint (fruit may be on another face / better seen by the other camera), then calls
  `pipe.note_reobserved(cam, track_id)`. **An `unknown` cube is never picked.**

## 4. Deploy to Jetson Orin Nano
```bash
yolo/bin/python deployment/export_set2_onnx.py        # dev box: portable ONNX
# ON the Jetson (engines are device-specific):
python deployment/build_set2_tensorrt.py --half       # detector + classifier FP16 engines
python deployment/run_perception.py --set set2 --target <fruit> --left 0 --right 1
```
`run_perception.py` auto-uses `best.engine`/`best.onnx` when present (TensorRT EP → CUDA → CPU).
Both nets are tiny (YOLO11n ~2.6 M; MobileNetV3-Small ~2.5 M), batch=1; the classifier runs only
on the few close/large crops per frame, so detector latency dominates. FP16 ≈ 2× throughput with
negligible accuracy loss on Orin Nano.

## 5. Validate, tune, and close the loop
- **Detector**: recall first (esp. white-face-only, small/distant, near-wall, partially occluded
  cubes), then mAP50 / mAP50-95; watch false positives on bright floor / walls.
- **Classifier**: overall acc, per-class precision/recall, confusion matrix, and the gate metrics
  in `confusion.txt`. Tune for **unknown-leak ≈ 0** first, then fruit-confusion, accepting lower
  recall.
- **Threshold tuning**: raise `conf_threshold` / `margin_threshold` (and `conflict_reject`) until
  the validation **false-pickup rate** (a non-target reaching `PICKUP_READY`) is ~0; the cost is
  more `unknown`/re-observe/skip — exactly the right trade for the −40/0 payoff. `min_bbox_px` /
  `pickup_min_bbox_px` set how close the robot must be before it trusts the fruit reading.
- **Multi-frame fusion**: the tracker keeps a `vote_window` of calibrated (conf, margin) per track;
  the policy uses count-based voting with a hard conflict-reject. EMA/confidence-weighting can be
  swapped in inside `_votes` if needed.
- **Sim→real**: pretrain on synthetic, then fine-tune both nets on real `left_camera`/`right_camera`
  captures — clear fruit per class, **white-face-only** cubes, Set 1 cubes/polyhedra, far/near,
  near-wall, low/bright light, motion blur, and ambiguous crops labelled `unknown`. Feed deployment
  failure logs (`runtime_logs/set2/`, saved on `unknown`/low-margin) back as new `unknown`/hard
  examples and re-train. Correct `unknown` labels carefully — that class carries the conservatism.

> Assumptions to confirm against the real objects (edit `configs/set2.yaml`): cube edge
> `cubes.size_m` (default 0.06 m), and whether the fruit faces are a **fixed** layout
> (`cubes.fixed_face_layout: true` to match the real cubes exactly) or vary. Drop the real
> cube STL in as `assets.cube_usd` if you prefer it over the procedural box. Robot-body
> occlusion proxies are off by default (`robot.occlusion_proxy: false`) as in Set 1.

lines: failure logs (`runtime_logs/set2/`, saved on `unknown`/low-margin) back as new `unknown`/hard
examples and re-train. Correct `unknown` labels carefully — that class carries the conservatism.
-> Assumptions to confirm against the real objects (edit `configs/set2.yaml`): cube edge
-> `cubes.size_m` (default 0.06 m), and whether the fruit faces are a **fixed** layout
-> (`cubes.fixed_face_layout: true` to match the real cubes exactly) or vary. Drop the real
-> cube STL in as `assets.cube_usd` if you prefer it over the procedural box. Robot-body
-> occlusion proxies are off by default (`robot.occlusion_proxy: false`) as in Set 1.
+> **Cube body**: the generator builds a clean procedural box at `cubes.size_m` (0.08 m).
+> A real cube STL is **not needed** — a fruit cube is geometrically just a box, and the
+> procedural body is what the decal placement + analytic projection math assume (a unit
+> cube). Leave `assets.cube_usd: null`. (Converting your cube STL would normalize it to a
+> different size and break that math, for no visual gain.)
+> **Fruit images** (`assets/fruit_textures/<fruit>/*.png|jpg`): each image should look like
+> the label as the camera will see it on the cube — the fruit filling a roughly **square**,
+> **opaque** frame (transparency is ignored by the renderer, so flatten onto the real sticker
+> background). Several variants per class improve robustness. avif/webp are not readable by the
+> USD texture loader — convert to png/jpg.
+> **Fruit-face layout** (`cubes.fixed_face_layout`): leave it `false`. The classifier reads
+> *visible pixels*, never which physical face the fruit is on, and the cube is tumbled through

+> is no need to match the real cube's exact face arrangement. Robot-body occlusion proxies are
+> off by default (`robot.occlusion_proxy: false`), as in Set 1.

content.text:
Done. Here's everything, including answers to each of your questions.

## What I just changed
- **Cube size → 0.08 m** in [set2.yaml](configs/set2.yaml#L?) (`cubes.size_m`).
- **Cleaned your texture folder**: converted the 4 `.avif`/`.webp` files to `.png` (flattening transparency onto white) and **deleted my 24 placeholder images**. You now have **7 real images per class**, all `.png`/`.jpg`. This matters because (a) Isaac's USD texture loader can't read avif/webp, and (b) you don't want my fake clipart polluting training.

## 1. The cube USD / convert_stl_to_usd / robocopy workflow
**For Set 2 you do *not* need to convert your cube STL at all.** A fruit cube is geometrically just a box, so the generator builds a clean **procedural box** at 0.08 m (`assets.cube_usd: null`). Keep it null — the fruit-decal placement and the analytic visibility math assume that unit box, and referencing a converted STL would resize it and break that math for zero visual benefit.

`convert_stl_to_usd.py` only matters for the **non-cube negatives** (octahedron/dodecahedron/icosahedron) that Set 2 *optionally* reuses as hard negatives. The workflow:
- **Isaac runs on Windows**, so generation happens there. The `isaac/assets/usd/*.usd` you made on Windows for Set 1 are already on the Windows copy — no copy-back needed to generate.
- robocopy WSL→Windows (now carries `assets/fruit_textures/` with your real images), then run `python sim\generate_set2_data.py --frames 9000`, then robocopy only `datasets\set2` back to WSL for training.
- If those polyhedra USDs aren't present on Windows, Set 2 just **skips negatives** — totally fine, not required.

## 2. "Fixed vs random fruit faces" — you can ignore it; leave it `false`
This setting only chooses *which 3 of the 6 faces* get the fruit decal. **It does not matter for the model.** The classifier learns from the *visible pixels*, never from which physical face the fruit sits on, and the generator already tumbles each cube through all orientations every frame. So `false` (random faces) just gives a bit more viewpoint diversity. There is **no need to match the real cube's exact face arrangement** — I over-stated that in the first README; it's now corrected.

## 3. What form the fruit images should take
They're used as **printed/sticker decals** laid on a cube face, so each image should look like the label *as the camera will see it on the real cube*:
- **Format:** `.png` or `.jpg` (you're set). Not avif/webp.
- **Opaque** — transparency is ignored by the renderer, so the image's background becomes the sticker's background. Use the real sticker background (usually white).
- **Roughly square**, with the **fruit filling most of the frame** (the decal quad is square, so wide/tall images get stretched and off-center fruit looks small).
- **Several variants per class** (different photos/angles) → more robustness. 7 each is good.
- Placeholders/real images are interchangeable to the pipeline — for best sim→real results, ideally the images resemble whatever is actually printed on the competition cubes; otherwise pretrain on these and fine-tune on real camera captures later.

One caveat worth knowing: these synthetic decals are an approximation of the real printed labels. The conservative `unknown`-first policy and the planned real-data fine-tuning step are what close that gap — so don't over-invest in perfect textures before you've seen the real cubes.

aiTitle: Set 2 cube detection and fruit classification pipeline

content.text: Then please tell me what to do next in Korean

아래 순서대로 진행하시면 됩니다. 코드·설정·과일 이미지는 모두 준비됐습니다.

## 1단계 — 데이터 생성 (Windows, Isaac Sim)
WSL → Windows로 복사 후 Isaac 파이썬으로 실행합니다. **STL 변환은 Set 2에 필요 없습니다** (절차적 박스 사용).

```powershell
robocopy \\wsl.localhost\Ubuntu-22.04\home\user\joon C:\joon /E /XD yolo runs datasets
$ISAAC = "C:\Users\user\Documents\IsaacSim\python.bat"
cd C:\joon
& $ISAAC sim\generate_set2_data.py --frames 9000
```

먼저 `--frames 200` 정도로 짧게 돌려서 결과를 확인하는 걸 권장합니다.

## 2단계 — 생성 결과 확인 (중요)
다음을 눈으로 점검하세요:
- `datasets/set2/detector/images/train/` — 큐브가 흰 박스로, 과일이 **3면에만** 인쇄 스티커처럼 보이는지
- `datasets/set2/classifier/train/` — `apple/orange/banana/pineapple` 폴더엔 **과일이 실제로 보이는** 크롭만, `unknown` 폴더엔 흰 면·잘림·작은 큐브가 들어갔는지
- 콘솔의 `[SET2] frame ... fruit_crops=...` 로그로 과일 라벨이 제대로 붙는지

과일이 너무 안 보이거나(`unknown`만 많음) 너무 헐겁게 붙으면 `configs/set2.yaml`의 `labeling.min_fruit_area_ratio`, `min_fruit_face_facing`를 조정합니다.

## 3단계 — 학습 데이터 복사 (Windows → WSL)
```powershell
robocopy C:\joon\datasets\set2 \\wsl.localhost\Ubuntu-22.04\home\user\joon\datasets\set2 /E
```

## 4단계 — 학습 (WSL, yolo 가상환경)
```bash
yolo/bin/python training/train_set2_detector.py   --epochs 120 --batch 32
yolo/bin/python training/train_set2_classifier.py --epochs 70  --imgsz 128
```

## 5단계 — 평가·임계값 튜닝
- `models/set2/classifier/confusion.txt`에서 **`unknown_leak_rate`(쓰레기를 과일로 오인 → −40 유발)**를 0에 가깝게 만드는 것이 1순위입니다.
- 그다음 `fruit_confusion_rate`. 검증셋 오집기(false pickup)가 ~0이 될 때까지 `set2.yaml`의 `runtime.conf_threshold`(0.75)·`margin_threshold`(0.15)를 올립니다. 재현율은 좀 떨어져도 됩니다.

## 6단계 — 배포 (Jetson Orin Nano)
```bash
# 개발 PC: ONNX
yolo/bin/python deployment/export_set2_onnx.py
# Jetson 위에서: 엔진 빌드 + 실행
python deployment/build_set2_tensorrt.py --half
python deployment/run_perception.py --set set2 --target banana --left 0 --right 1 --show
```

## 7단계 — 실데이터 미세조정 (대회 직전)
실제 카메라로 각 과일·**흰 면만 보이는 큐브**·애매한 크롭을 찍어 `unknown` 위주로 라벨링 → 두 모델 fine-tune. 합성으로 사전학습, 실데이터로 보정하는 흐름입니다.

---

지금 바로 할 일은 **1단계(짧게 200프레임)** → **2단계 육안 확인**입니다. 합성 이미지가 실제 큐브와 얼마나 비슷한지가 전체 성능을 좌우하므로, 여기서 한 번 보여주시면 라벨 임계값이나 텍스처 표현을 같이 조정해 드리겠습니다.
세트2에는 정육면체 큐브만 있을 예정이라, 지금 보이는 이미지에 있는 다른 모형들은 제외하고 과일사진 부착된 큐브만 있도록 수정해줘

네, 세트2 아레나에는 **과일이 붙은 큐브만** 남기고, 다른 모형(정팔면체·정십이면체·정이십면체)과 무지(흰색) 큐브를 제거하도록 수정하겠습니다.

먼저 설정에서 비큐브 네거티브를 끄고, 무지 큐브 개수를 0으로 바꿉니다.

input.old_string:
count_range: [1, 4]          # cubes present per frame
  n_plain: 3                   # plain (no-fruit) white cubes in the pool -> 'unknown' crops
input.new_string:
count_range: [1, 4]          # cubes present per frame
  n_plain: 0                   # plain (no-fruit) white cubes; 0 = only fruit cubes in the arena
                               # (white-face 'unknown' crops still come from fruit cubes' 3 plain faces)

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# ============================================================================
# Set 2 perception config - white cubes with fruit images on 3 of 6 faces, in
# the same 4x4 m arena. SEPARATE from Set 1 (own dataset, models, thresholds,
# runtime policy). Every tunable for the Isaac Sim data generation lives here so
# the sim files stay generic. Edit values to match the real cubes / arena.
# ============================================================================

set: set2

classes:
  fruits: [apple, orange, banana, pineapple]   # classifier fruit classes
  detector: [cube_candidate]                   # detector: single class
  unknown: unknown                             # classifier reject class

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- assets
# Fruit images used as randomized printed/sticker labels. Drop multiple real
# photos per class into these folders (png/jpg). sim/make_fruit_textures.py can
# generate distinguishable PLACEHOLDERS so the pipeline runs before real images
# arrive - replace them with real fruit photos for sim->real quality.
assets:
  fruit_texture_dir: assets/fruit_textures   # <dir>/<fruit>/*.png|jpg
  # Optional: a cube body USD (e.g. converted from a real cube STL). If null/missing
  # the generator builds a clean procedural box (preferred for per-face texturing).
  cube_usd: null

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- arena (shared shape with set1)
arena:
  size_x: 4.0
  size_y: 4.0
  wall_height: 0.5
  wall_thickness: 0.05
  floor_color: [0.45, 0.30, 0.17]
  floor_color_jitter: 0.08
  floor_roughness: [0.5, 0.9]
  wall_color: [0.92, 0.92, 0.92]
  wall_color_jitter: 0.06
  wall_roughness: [0.4, 0.8]
  spawn_margin: 0.35

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- camera (NUROUM V11) - identical rig to set1
camera:
  width: 1280
  height: 720
  hfov_deg: 90.0
  focal_length: 10.4775
  horizontal_aperture: 20.955
  fx: 640.0
  fy: 640.0
  cx: 640.0
  cy: 360.0

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- robot sensor rig (identical to set1)
robot:
  base_height: 0.0
  cam_height: 0.20
  cam_forward_offset: 0.10
  cam_lateral_offset: 0.15
  cam_pitch_deg: -20.0
  cam_yaw_outward_deg: 30.0
  lidar_height: 0.30
  occlusion_proxy: false
  jitter:
    height: [-0.01, 0.01]
    pitch_deg: [-7.0, 7.0]     # -> effective pitch -13..-27 deg
    yaw_deg: [-3.0, 3.0]
    roll_deg: [-2.0, 2.0]

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- cubes / placement
cubes:
  size_m: 0.08                 # real cube edge length (8 cm)
  size_jitter_m: 0.005         # per-frame edge variation
  count_range: [1, 4]          # cubes present per frame
  n_plain: 3                   # plain (no-fruit) white cubes in the pool -> 'unknown' crops
  z_on_floor: true
  fixed_face_layout: false     # true -> fruit always on the same 3 faces (real object);
                               # false -> randomize which 3 faces per cube (robustness)
  fruit_faces: 3               # exactly 3 of 6 faces carry fruit
  # White 3D-printed plastic body (UsdPreviewSurface).
  body_material:
    base_color: [0.88, 0.88, 0.88]
    color_jitter: 0.06
    roughness: [0.35, 0.7]
    metallic: 0.0
  # Fraction of cubes that are DISTRACTORS (no fruit at all -> always 'unknown'):
  #   plain white cubes + (if Set 1 USDs present) non-cube polyhedra.
  distractor_frac: 0.25
  set1_usd_dir: isaac/assets/usd      # reuse octa/dodeca/icosa as non-cube negatives if present
  neg_size_m: 0.08                    # rendered size of non-cube negatives
  neg_calib_m: 64.9                   # set1 render_calib quirk: rep scale 1.0 renders USD at ~64.9 m

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- fruit label (printed/sticker) realism
# A fruit image is rendered as a thin textured decal sitting flush on a face,
# simulating a printed/sticker label rather than a clean digital texture.
fruit_label:
  scale_frac: [0.55, 0.92]     # label size as fraction of the face edge
  offset_frac: 0.12            # random center offset on the face (+-frac of edge)
  rotation_deg: [-8, 8]        # in-plane misalignment
  border_frac: [0.0, 0.10]     # paper/sticker white border (fraction of label)
  raise_eps_m: 0.0008          # lift above the face to avoid z-fighting
  # Printed-look colour/exposure jitter applied via the texture scale/bias inputs:
  brightness: [0.8, 1.15]
  contrast: [0.85, 1.15]
  saturation: [0.8, 1.15]
  glare_prob: 0.25             # chance of a brighter specular label material
  roughness: [0.3, 0.7]

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- lighting / DR (shared with set1)
lighting:
  sun_intensity: [2000, 9000]
  sun_pitch_deg: [-70, -20]
  sun_yaw_deg: [-180, 180]
  dome_intensity: [600, 2500]
  color_temp: [4000, 8000]

postprocess:
  motion_blur: true
  gaussian_noise: true
  exposure_jitter: true

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- dataset output
dataset:
  root: datasets/set2
  detector_subdir: detector
  classifier_subdir: classifier
  metadata_subdir: metadata
  val_ratio: 0.15
  min_frame_brightness: 12

# Rules that turn a detected cube into a classifier sample vs `unknown`. The label
# is based on VISIBLE FRUIT EVIDENCE (analytic face-facing + projected fruit area),
# never on the cube's hidden physical identity.
labeling:
  detector_min_box_px: 8          # detector keeps boxes down to here (high recall)
  min_box_px: 36                  # min(w,h) px for a crop to be eligible for a fruit label
  min_visible_frac: 0.55          # 1 - occlusionRatio of the cube box
  max_truncation_px: 2            # cube box this close to the image border = truncated
  # Fruit-evidence gates (ALL must hold for a fruit-class label, else -> unknown):
  min_fruit_face_facing: 0.30     # max over fruit faces of (-normal . view_dir), 0..1
  min_fruit_area_ratio: 0.10      # visible fruit pixel area / cube box pixel area
  min_fruit_box_px: 14            # min(w,h) px of the visible fruit region
  # Classifier crop augmentation (simulate detector imperfection):
  crop_margin_frac: [-0.08, 0.20]
  crop_shift_frac: 0.10
  crops_per_object: 2
  # Hard-but-identifiable share: keep some borderline-but-clear fruit views as the
  # fruit class (not unknown) so the classifier still recognizes harder real views.
  hard_positive_keep: 0.5
  # Extra explicit unknown sources:
  background_unknown_frac: 0.3    # frames that also emit a background crop
  whiteface_unknown_boost: true   # white-face-only cube views -> unknown (the core case)

render:
  rt_subframes: 32

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- runtime (Jetson) - conservative, false-positive averse
runtime:
  detector_conf: 0.25
  detector_imgsz: 640
  # Only classify when the cube is close/large enough for reliable fruit reading:
  min_bbox_px: 48               # min(w,h) px (larger than set1: fruit detail needs resolution)
  min_bbox_area_ratio: 0.006
  reject_truncation_px: 3
  classifier_imgsz: 128
  # Conservative fruit acceptance (wrong pickup = -40, miss = 0):
  conf_threshold: 0.75          # calibrated softmax prob for top-1 fruit
  margin_threshold: 0.15        # top1 - top2 probability
  unknown_conf_relax: 0.50      # below this top-1 conf, treat as unknown regardless of class
  # Tracking + voting:
  track_iou: 0.3
  track_max_age: 12
  vote_window: 9
  min_confirmations: 3          # target-fruit votes needed for TARGET_CONFIRMED
  max_reobserve: 3              # active viewpoint changes for an unknown/uncertain cube
  unknown_patience: 4           # consecutive unknowns before requesting a viewpoint change
  # PICKUP_READY gating:
  pickup_min_bbox_px: 110       # cube must be close (large) to pick
  reconfirm_within: 3           # re-seen as target within N recent frames
  conflict_reject: 2            # >=N strong non-target-fruit votes -> REJECT (never pick)

lines: size_m: 0.08                 # real cube edge length (8 cm)

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# ============================================================================
# Set 2 perception config - white cubes with fruit images on 3 of 6 faces, in
# the same 4x4 m arena. SEPARATE from Set 1 (own dataset, models, thresholds,
# runtime policy). Every tunable for the Isaac Sim data generation lives here so
# the sim files stay generic. Edit values to match the real cubes / arena.
# ============================================================================

set: set2

classes:
  fruits: [apple, orange, banana, pineapple]   # classifier fruit classes
  detector: [cube_candidate]                   # detector: single class
  unknown: unknown                             # classifier reject class

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- assets
# Fruit images used as randomized printed/sticker labels. Drop multiple real
# photos per class into these folders (png/jpg). sim/make_fruit_textures.py can
# generate distinguishable PLACEHOLDERS so the pipeline runs before real images
# arrive - replace them with real fruit photos for sim->real quality.
assets:
  fruit_texture_dir: assets/fruit_textures   # <dir>/<fruit>/*.png|jpg
  # Optional: a cube body USD (e.g. converted from a real cube STL). If null/missing
  # the generator builds a clean procedural box (preferred for per-face texturing).
  cube_usd: null

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- arena (shared shape with set1)
arena:
  size_x: 4.0
  size_y: 4.0
  wall_height: 0.5
  wall_thickness: 0.05
  floor_color: [0.45, 0.30, 0.17]
  floor_color_jitter: 0.08
  floor_roughness: [0.5, 0.9]
  wall_color: [0.92, 0.92, 0.92]
  wall_color_jitter: 0.06
  wall_roughness: [0.4, 0.8]
  spawn_margin: 0.35

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- camera (NUROUM V11) - identical rig to set1
camera:
  width: 1280
  height: 720
  hfov_deg: 90.0
  focal_length: 10.4775
  horizontal_aperture: 20.955
  fx: 640.0
  fy: 640.0
  cx: 640.0
  cy: 360.0

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- robot sensor rig (identical to set1)
robot:
  base_height: 0.0
  cam_height: 0.20
  cam_forward_offset: 0.10
  cam_lateral_offset: 0.15
  cam_pitch_deg: -20.0
  cam_yaw_outward_deg: 30.0
  lidar_height: 0.30
  occlusion_proxy: false
  jitter:
    height: [-0.01, 0.01]
    pitch_deg: [-7.0, 7.0]     # -> effective pitch -13..-27 deg
    yaw_deg: [-3.0, 3.0]
    roll_deg: [-2.0, 2.0]

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- cubes / placement
cubes:
  size_m: 0.08                 # real cube edge length (8 cm)
  size_jitter_m: 0.005         # per-frame edge variation
  count_range: [1, 4]          # cubes present per frame
  n_plain: 0                   # plain (no-fruit) white cubes; 0 = only fruit cubes in the arena
                               # (white-face 'unknown' crops still come from fruit cubes' 3 plain faces)
  z_on_floor: true
  fixed_face_layout: false     # true -> fruit always on the same 3 faces (real object);
                               # false -> randomize which 3 faces per cube (robustness)
  fruit_faces: 3               # exactly 3 of 6 faces carry fruit
  # White 3D-printed plastic body (UsdPreviewSurface).
  body_material:
    base_color: [0.88, 0.88, 0.88]
    color_jitter: 0.06
    roughness: [0.35, 0.7]
    metallic: 0.0
  # Fraction of cubes that are DISTRACTORS (no fruit at all -> always 'unknown'):
  #   plain white cubes + (if Set 1 USDs present) non-cube polyhedra.
  distractor_frac: 0.25
  set1_usd_dir: isaac/assets/usd      # reuse octa/dodeca/icosa as non-cube negatives if present
  neg_size_m: 0.08                    # rendered size of non-cube negatives
  neg_calib_m: 64.9                   # set1 render_calib quirk: rep scale 1.0 renders USD at ~64.9 m

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- fruit label (printed/sticker) realism
# A fruit image is rendered as a thin textured decal sitting flush on a face,
# simulating a printed/sticker label rather than a clean digital texture.
fruit_label:
  scale_frac: [0.55, 0.92]     # label size as fraction of the face edge
  offset_frac: 0.12            # random center offset on the face (+-frac of edge)
  rotation_deg: [-8, 8]        # in-plane misalignment
  border_frac: [0.0, 0.10]     # paper/sticker white border (fraction of label)
  raise_eps_m: 0.0008          # lift above the face to avoid z-fighting
  # Printed-look colour/exposure jitter applied via the texture scale/bias inputs:
  brightness: [0.8, 1.15]
  contrast: [0.85, 1.15]
  saturation: [0.8, 1.15]
  glare_prob: 0.25             # chance of a brighter specular label material
  roughness: [0.3, 0.7]

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- lighting / DR (shared with set1)
lighting:
  sun_intensity: [2000, 9000]
  sun_pitch_deg: [-70, -20]
  sun_yaw_deg: [-180, 180]
  dome_intensity: [600, 2500]
  color_temp: [4000, 8000]

postprocess:
  motion_blur: true
  gaussian_noise: true
  exposure_jitter: true

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- dataset output
dataset:
  root: datasets/set2
  detector_subdir: detector
  classifier_subdir: classifier
  metadata_subdir: metadata
  val_ratio: 0.15
  min_frame_brightness: 12

# Rules that turn a detected cube into a classifier sample vs `unknown`. The label
# is based on VISIBLE FRUIT EVIDENCE (analytic face-facing + projected fruit area),
# never on the cube's hidden physical identity.
labeling:
  detector_min_box_px: 8          # detector keeps boxes down to here (high recall)
  min_box_px: 36                  # min(w,h) px for a crop to be eligible for a fruit label
  min_visible_frac: 0.55          # 1 - occlusionRatio of the cube box
  max_truncation_px: 2            # cube box this close to the image border = truncated
  # Fruit-evidence gates (ALL must hold for a fruit-class label, else -> unknown):
  min_fruit_face_facing: 0.30     # max over fruit faces of (-normal . view_dir), 0..1
  min_fruit_area_ratio: 0.10      # visible fruit pixel area / cube box pixel area
  min_fruit_box_px: 14            # min(w,h) px of the visible fruit region
  # Classifier crop augmentation (simulate detector imperfection):
  crop_margin_frac: [-0.08, 0.20]
  crop_shift_frac: 0.10
  crops_per_object: 2
  # Hard-but-identifiable share: keep some borderline-but-clear fruit views as the
  # fruit class (not unknown) so the classifier still recognizes harder real views.
  hard_positive_keep: 0.5
  # Extra explicit unknown sources:
  background_unknown_frac: 0.3    # frames that also emit a background crop
  whiteface_unknown_boost: true   # white-face-only cube views -> unknown (the core case)

render:
  rt_subframes: 32

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- runtime (Jetson) - conservative, false-positive averse
runtime:
  detector_conf: 0.25
  detector_imgsz: 640
  # Only classify when the cube is close/large enough for reliable fruit reading:
  min_bbox_px: 48               # min(w,h) px (larger than set1: fruit detail needs resolution)
  min_bbox_area_ratio: 0.006
  reject_truncation_px: 3
  classifier_imgsz: 128
  # Conservative fruit acceptance (wrong pickup = -40, miss = 0):
  conf_threshold: 0.75          # calibrated softmax prob for top-1 fruit
  margin_threshold: 0.15        # top1 - top2 probability
  unknown_conf_relax: 0.50      # below this top-1 conf, treat as unknown regardless of class
  # Tracking + voting:
  track_iou: 0.3
  track_max_age: 12
  vote_window: 9
  min_confirmations: 3          # target-fruit votes needed for TARGET_CONFIRMED
  max_reobserve: 3              # active viewpoint changes for an unknown/uncertain cube
  unknown_patience: 4           # consecutive unknowns before requesting a viewpoint change
  # PICKUP_READY gating:
  pickup_min_bbox_px: 110       # cube must be close (large) to pick
  reconfirm_within: 3           # re-seen as target within N recent frames
  conflict_reject: 2            # >=N strong non-target-fruit votes -> REJECT (never pick)

content.text:
수정 완료했습니다.

## 변경 내용
- **다른 모형 제거**: 정팔면체·정십이면체·정이십면체(비큐브 네거티브)를 기본적으로 끔 → `cubes.use_noncube_negatives: false`. 생성기가 해당 USD를 아예 로드하지 않습니다.
- **무지(흰색) 큐브 제거**: `cubes.n_plain: 0` → 풀에 과일 큐브 12개(클래스당 3개)만 남습니다.
- 결과적으로 **씬에는 과일이 붙은 큐브만** 등장합니다.

## 확인 사항 (중요)
`unknown` 학습 크롭은 이제 다음에서 나옵니다:
- **과일 큐브의 흰 면(3면) 뷰** — 과일이 안 보이는 각도. 이게 가장 큰 unknown 소스입니다.
- 배경 크롭(`labeling.background_unknown_frac: 0.3`)
- 애매한 경계 뷰

즉 무지 큐브를 빼도 unknown 데이터는 충분히 확보됩니다. 학습 코드에 클래스 불균형 보정(weighted sampler)도 이미 들어 있어 괜찮습니다.

## 다음 할 일
바뀐 설정(`configs/set2.yaml`, `sim/generate_set2_data.py`)을 Windows로 다시 복사한 뒤, 짧게 한 번 돌려서 **다른 모형이 사라지고 과일 큐브만 보이는지** 확인하세요:

```powershell
robocopy \\wsl.localhost\Ubuntu-22.04\home\user\joon C:\joon /E /XD yolo runs datasets
```

content.text:
정리 끝났습니다. 이제 본 생성으로 넘어가시면 됩니다.

## 본 생성 전 체크 (1줄 요약)
- WSL: 깨끗함 ✅ / 설정: `size_m 0.08`, `n_plain 0`, `use_noncube_negatives false` ✅ / 텍스처: 클래스당 7개 ✅

## 진행 순서

**1) 최신 코드/설정을 Windows로 복사**
```powershell
robocopy \\wsl.localhost\Ubuntu-22.04\home\user\joon C:\joon /E /XD yolo runs datasets
```

**2) Windows에 남은 200프레임 테스트 데이터 삭제** (섞이지 않도록 — 이것만 잊지 마세요)
```powershell
Remove-Item -Recurse -Force C:\joon\datasets\set2
```

**3) 본 생성**
```powershell
$ISAAC = "C:\Users\user\Documents\IsaacSim\python.bat"
cd C:\joon
& $ISAAC sim\generate_set2_data.py --frames 9000
```

**4) 학습 데이터만 WSL로 회수**
```powershell
robocopy C:\joon\datasets\set2 \\wsl.localhost\Ubuntu-22.04\home\user\joon\datasets\set2 /E
```

생성이 끝나면 클래스별 크롭 개수(`datasets/set2/classifier/train/<class>` 파일 수)를 알려주세요. `unknown` 비중이 과도하거나 특정 과일이 너무 적으면 라벨 임계값(`min_fruit_area_ratio` 등)을 같이 조정한 뒤 학습으로 넘어가겠습니다.
5개 클래스 다 정상적으로 생성되었고 비중도 괜찮은 것 같아
좋습니다. 학습으로 넘어가기 전에 WSL에 데이터가 잘 들어왔는지와 클래스 분포만 빠르게 확인하겠습니다.

echo ; echo "=== classifier 크롭 분포 ===" ; for s in train val; do echo "[$s]"; for c in apple orange banana pineapple unknown; do printf "  %-10s %s\n" "$c" "$(ls datasets/set2/classifier/$s/$c 2>/dev/null|wc -l)"; done; done
echo ; echo "=== metadata 수 ===" ; ls datasets/set2/metadata 2>/dev/null | wc -l

content.text:
데이터 정상입니다. 분포도 학습에 적합합니다.

- **Detector**: train 8,502 / val 1,418 (이미지·라벨 수 일치 ✅)
- **Classifier**: 과일 클래스당 ~2,500개, `unknown` ~18,500개. unknown이 큰 건 의도된 것이고(보수적 동작의 핵심), 학습 코드의 가중 샘플러가 자동으로 균형을 맞춥니다 ✅

## 학습 단계 (WSL, yolo 가상환경)

두 모델은 독립적이라 순서대로 돌리면 됩니다.

**1) 큐브 디텍터 (YOLO11n)**
```bash
yolo/bin/python training/train_set2_detector.py --epochs 120 --batch 32
```

**2) 과일 분류기 (MobileNetV3-Small)**
```bash
yolo/bin/python training/train_set2_classifier.py --epochs 70 --imgsz 128
```

## 끝나고 꼭 볼 것
- **Detector**: 출력 로그의 `recall`(작은/먼/벽쪽 큐브 놓치지 않는지 우선), 그다음 `mAP50`.
- **Classifier**: `models/set2/classifier/confusion.txt`에서
  - **`unknown_leak_rate`** (쓰레기를 과일로 오인 → −40 유발) → **0에 가깝게**가 최우선
  - `fruit_confusion_rate`, `identifiable_fruit_recall`

학습은 GPU로 수십 분~수 시간 걸리니 직접 돌리시면서 지켜보시는 걸 권합니다. 제가 백그라운드로 돌려드리길 원하시면 말씀해 주세요. 끝난 뒤 `confusion.txt`와 디텍터 metric 로그를 붙여주시면 임계값 튜닝(`conf_threshold`, `margin_threshold` 등)을 같이 잡아드리겠습니다.

분류기가 먼저 학습 완료됐어
best val_acc=0.8032  temperature=1.041
confusion matrix (rows=true, cols=pred)
        apple   banana  orange  pineapple       unknown
apple   367     4       7       7       71
banana  3       318     15      15      75
orange  4       9       320     10      77
pineapple       1       16      8       369     44
unknown 158     132     114     181     2508
apple      precision=0.689 recall=0.805
banana     precision=0.664 recall=0.746
orange     precision=0.690 recall=0.762
pineapple  precision=0.634 recall=0.842
unknown    precision=0.904 recall=0.811
--- at runtime gate conf>=0.75 margin>=0.15 ---
unknown_leak_rate (junk accepted as fruit) = 267/3093 = 0.0863   <-- drives -40 penalties, push toward 0
fruit_confusion_rate (wrong fruit accepted) = 12/1129 = 0.0106
identifiable_fruit_recall = 0.6420

아주 좋은 신호들과 한 가지 손볼 곳이 보입니다.

**좋은 점**
- `temperature=1.041` → 모델이 거의 잘 보정돼 있어 confidence를 믿고 임계값 잡기 좋습니다.
- `fruit_confusion_rate=0.0106` → 과일끼리 헷갈려 **엉뚱한 과일**을 집을 위험은 거의 없음 (아주 중요).
- 과일 오류의 대부분이 `unknown` 방향(예: apple 71개가 unknown으로) → 안전한 방향의 실수.

**손볼 곳**
- `unknown_leak_rate=0.0863` → 쓰레기/흰면 크롭의 8.6%가 과일로 통과. −40 위험과 직결되니 더 낮춰야 합니다. (단, 이건 *프레임 1장* 기준이고, 런타임 정책은 3프레임 연속 일치 + 충돌검사를 요구하므로 실제 집기 오류는 훨씬 낮습니다.)
- 가장 싼 해결책은 **재학습 없이 런타임 임계값을 올리는 것**입니다. 지금 리포트는 conf≥0.75/margin≥0.15 고정값인데, 실제 최적점을 찾기 위해 검증셋으로 스윕해 보겠습니다.

학습된 모델 + 검증셋이 WSL에 있으니 바로 임계값 스윕을 돌리겠습니다.

input.command:
yolo/bin/python - <<'PY'
import json, os, numpy as np, torch, torch.nn as nn
from torchvision import datasets, transforms, models
from torch.utils.data import DataLoader

cdir="models/set2/classifier"
classes=json.load(open(f"{cdir}/classes.json"))
meta=json.load(open(f"{cdir}/temperature.json")); T=meta["temperature"]; imgsz=meta["imgsz"]
dev="cuda" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu"
m=models.mobilenet_v3_small(weights=None)
m.classifier[3]=nn.Linear(m.classifier[3].in_features,len(classes))
m.load_state_dict(torch.load(f"{cdir}/best.pt",map_location=dev)); m.to(dev).eval()
tf=transforms.Compose([transforms.Resize((imgsz,imgsz)),transforms.ToTensor(),
    transforms.Normalize([0.485,0.456,0.406],[0.229,0.224,0.225])])
val=datasets.ImageFolder("datasets/set2/classifier/val",tf)
dl=DataLoader(val,batch_size=256,num_workers=8)
P=[];Y=[]
with torch.no_grad():
    for x,y in dl:
        z=m(x.to(dev))/T; P.append(torch.softmax(z,1).cpu().numpy()); Y.append(y.numpy())
P=np.concatenate(P); Y=np.concatenate(Y)
fruits={"apple","banana","orange","pineapple"}; unk=classes.index("unknown")
order=np.argsort(-P,1); top1=order[:,0]; conf=P[np.arange(len(P)),top1]
margin=conf-P[np.arange(len(P)),order[:,1]]
is_fruit=np.array([classes[i] in fruits for i in top1])
true_unk=(Y==unk); true_fruit=np.array([classes[t] in fruits for t in Y])
print(f"val crops: {len(Y)}  true_unknown={true_unk.sum()}  true_fruit={true_fruit.sum()}")
print(f"{'conf':>5}{'margin':>7}{'leak%':>8}{'fruit_recall%':>15}{'fconf%':>8}")
for c in [0.75,0.80,0.85,0.90,0.92,0.95]:
    for mg in [0.15,0.25,0.35,0.50]:
        acc=(conf>=c)&(margin>=mg)
        af=acc&is_fruit
        leak=(af&true_unk).sum()/max(true_unk.sum(),1)
        rec=(af&(top1==Y)&true_fruit).sum()/max(true_fruit.sum(),1)
        fc=(af&(top1!=Y)&true_fruit).sum()/max((af&true_fruit).sum(),1)
        print(f"{c:>5}{mg:>7}{leak*100:>8.2f}{rec*100:>15.1f}{fc*100:>8.2f}")
PY
input.description: Sweep classifier confidence/margin thresholds on val set

aiTitle: Set 2 cube detection and fruit classification pipeline

content.text:
핵심 발견: **margin은 사실상 무의미**합니다(같은 conf에서 margin을 올려도 수치 불변 → 모델 분포가 뾰족해 top1이 항상 0.15 이상 앞섬). 그래서 조절 레버는 **conf_threshold 하나**입니다. 트레이드오프:

| conf | unknown leak%/프레임 | 과일 recall%/프레임 |
|---|---|---|
| 0.75 (현재) | 8.6 | 64 |
| 0.85 | 4.6 | 53 |
| **0.90** | **2.0** | **40** |
| 0.92 | 1.1 | 32 |
| 0.95 | 0.5 | 16 |

**왜 conf 0.90이 적절한가:** 위 leak은 *프레임 1장* 기준이고, 런타임 정책은 ① **3프레임 연속 같은 타깃** 일치 + ② 다른 과일 2표면 즉시 REJECT를 요구합니다. unknown 누출은 4개 과일에 거의 균등 분산(158/132/114/181)되므로, 쓰레기가 *같은 타깃으로 3번* 통과할 확률은 2%보다 훨씬 낮고, 분산되면 오히려 REJECT됩니다. 따라서 **실제 오집기 확률은 0에 수렴**합니다. recall 40%도 로봇이 접근하며 수십 프레임을 보고 또렷한 면에선 프레임 recall이 훨씬 높아 확정에 문제없습니다(놓침=0점이라 허용).

임계값을 0.90으로 올리겠습니다(재학습 불필요).

input.old_string:
# Conservative fruit acceptance (wrong pickup = -40, miss = 0):
  conf_threshold: 0.75          # calibrated softmax prob for top-1 fruit
  margin_threshold: 0.15        # top1 - top2 probability
  unknown_conf_relax: 0.50      # below this top-1 conf, treat as unknown regardless of class
input.new_string:
# Conservative fruit acceptance (wrong pickup = -40, miss = 0). Tuned from a val sweep:
  # at conf 0.90 the per-frame unknown-leak is ~2% (vs 8.6% at 0.75) and, combined with the
  # 3-frame confirmation + conflict-reject below, the effective false-pickup rate ~ 0.
  # margin barely binds (softmax is peaky) so it's just a cheap flat-distribution guard.
  conf_threshold: 0.90          # calibrated softmax prob for top-1 fruit
  margin_threshold: 0.10        # top1 - top2 probability
  unknown_conf_relax: 0.60      # below this top-1 conf, treat as unknown regardless of class

# Conservative fruit acceptance (wrong pickup = -40, miss = 0). Tuned from a val sweep:
  # at conf 0.90 the per-frame unknown-leak is ~2% (vs 8.6% at 0.75) and, combined with the
  # 3-frame confirmation + conflict-reject below, the effective false-pickup rate ~ 0.
  # margin barely binds (softmax is peaky) so it's just a cheap flat-distribution guard.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# ============================================================================
# Set 2 perception config - white cubes with fruit images on 3 of 6 faces, in
# the same 4x4 m arena. SEPARATE from Set 1 (own dataset, models, thresholds,
# runtime policy). Every tunable for the Isaac Sim data generation lives here so
# the sim files stay generic. Edit values to match the real cubes / arena.
# ============================================================================

set: set2

classes:
  fruits: [apple, orange, banana, pineapple]   # classifier fruit classes
  detector: [cube_candidate]                   # detector: single class
  unknown: unknown                             # classifier reject class

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- assets
# Fruit images used as randomized printed/sticker labels. Drop multiple real
# photos per class into these folders (png/jpg). sim/make_fruit_textures.py can
# generate distinguishable PLACEHOLDERS so the pipeline runs before real images
# arrive - replace them with real fruit photos for sim->real quality.
assets:
  fruit_texture_dir: assets/fruit_textures   # <dir>/<fruit>/*.png|jpg
  # Optional: a cube body USD (e.g. converted from a real cube STL). If null/missing
  # the generator builds a clean procedural box (preferred for per-face texturing).
  cube_usd: null

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- arena (shared shape with set1)
arena:
  size_x: 4.0
  size_y: 4.0
  wall_height: 0.5
  wall_thickness: 0.05
  floor_color: [0.45, 0.30, 0.17]
  floor_color_jitter: 0.08
  floor_roughness: [0.5, 0.9]
  wall_color: [0.92, 0.92, 0.92]
  wall_color_jitter: 0.06
  wall_roughness: [0.4, 0.8]
  spawn_margin: 0.35

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- camera (NUROUM V11) - identical rig to set1
camera:
  width: 1280
  height: 720
  hfov_deg: 90.0
  focal_length: 10.4775
  horizontal_aperture: 20.955
  fx: 640.0
  fy: 640.0
  cx: 640.0
  cy: 360.0

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- robot sensor rig (identical to set1)
robot:
  base_height: 0.0
  cam_height: 0.20
  cam_forward_offset: 0.10
  cam_lateral_offset: 0.15
  cam_pitch_deg: -20.0
  cam_yaw_outward_deg: 30.0
  lidar_height: 0.30
  occlusion_proxy: false
  jitter:
    height: [-0.01, 0.01]
    pitch_deg: [-7.0, 7.0]     # -> effective pitch -13..-27 deg
    yaw_deg: [-3.0, 3.0]
    roll_deg: [-2.0, 2.0]

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- cubes / placement
cubes:
  size_m: 0.08                 # real cube edge length (8 cm)
  size_jitter_m: 0.005         # per-frame edge variation
  count_range: [1, 4]          # cubes present per frame
  n_plain: 0                   # plain (no-fruit) white cubes; 0 = only fruit cubes in the arena
                               # (white-face 'unknown' crops still come from fruit cubes' 3 plain faces)
  z_on_floor: true
  fixed_face_layout: false     # true -> fruit always on the same 3 faces (real object);
                               # false -> randomize which 3 faces per cube (robustness)
  fruit_faces: 3               # exactly 3 of 6 faces carry fruit
  # White 3D-printed plastic body (UsdPreviewSurface).
  body_material:
    base_color: [0.88, 0.88, 0.88]
    color_jitter: 0.06
    roughness: [0.35, 0.7]
    metallic: 0.0
  # Set 2 arena contains ONLY fruit cubes. Non-cube polyhedra (Set 1 shapes) are NOT
  # present during the Set 2 mission, so they are disabled as scene negatives by default.
  use_noncube_negatives: false        # true -> add octa/dodeca/icosa as hard negatives
  set1_usd_dir: isaac/assets/usd      # where those USDs live (only used if the flag is true)
  neg_size_m: 0.08                    # rendered size of non-cube negatives (if enabled)
  neg_calib_m: 64.9                   # set1 render_calib quirk: rep scale 1.0 renders USD at ~64.9 m

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- fruit label (printed/sticker) realism
# A fruit image is rendered as a thin textured decal sitting flush on a face,
# simulating a printed/sticker label rather than a clean digital texture.
fruit_label:
  scale_frac: [0.55, 0.92]     # label size as fraction of the face edge
  offset_frac: 0.12            # random center offset on the face (+-frac of edge)
  rotation_deg: [-8, 8]        # in-plane misalignment
  border_frac: [0.0, 0.10]     # paper/sticker white border (fraction of label)
  raise_eps_m: 0.0008          # lift above the face to avoid z-fighting
  # Printed-look colour/exposure jitter applied via the texture scale/bias inputs:
  brightness: [0.8, 1.15]
  contrast: [0.85, 1.15]
  saturation: [0.8, 1.15]
  glare_prob: 0.25             # chance of a brighter specular label material
  roughness: [0.3, 0.7]

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- lighting / DR (shared with set1)
lighting:
  sun_intensity: [2000, 9000]
  sun_pitch_deg: [-70, -20]
  sun_yaw_deg: [-180, 180]
  dome_intensity: [600, 2500]
  color_temp: [4000, 8000]

postprocess:
  motion_blur: true
  gaussian_noise: true
  exposure_jitter: true

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- dataset output
dataset:
  root: datasets/set2
  detector_subdir: detector
  classifier_subdir: classifier
  metadata_subdir: metadata
  val_ratio: 0.15
  min_frame_brightness: 12

# Rules that turn a detected cube into a classifier sample vs `unknown`. The label
# is based on VISIBLE FRUIT EVIDENCE (analytic face-facing + projected fruit area),
# never on the cube's hidden physical identity.
labeling:
  detector_min_box_px: 8          # detector keeps boxes down to here (high recall)
  min_box_px: 36                  # min(w,h) px for a crop to be eligible for a fruit label
  min_visible_frac: 0.55          # 1 - occlusionRatio of the cube box
  max_truncation_px: 2            # cube box this close to the image border = truncated
  # Fruit-evidence gates (ALL must hold for a fruit-class label, else -> unknown):
  min_fruit_face_facing: 0.30     # max over fruit faces of (-normal . view_dir), 0..1
  min_fruit_area_ratio: 0.10      # visible fruit pixel area / cube box pixel area
  min_fruit_box_px: 14            # min(w,h) px of the visible fruit region
  # Classifier crop augmentation (simulate detector imperfection):
  crop_margin_frac: [-0.08, 0.20]
  crop_shift_frac: 0.10
  crops_per_object: 2
  # Hard-but-identifiable share: keep some borderline-but-clear fruit views as the
  # fruit class (not unknown) so the classifier still recognizes harder real views.
  hard_positive_keep: 0.5
  # Extra explicit unknown sources:
  background_unknown_frac: 0.3    # frames that also emit a background crop
  whiteface_unknown_boost: true   # white-face-only cube views -> unknown (the core case)

render:
  rt_subframes: 32

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- runtime (Jetson) - conservative, false-positive averse
runtime:
  detector_conf: 0.25
  detector_imgsz: 640
  # Only classify when the cube is close/large enough for reliable fruit reading:
  min_bbox_px: 48               # min(w,h) px (larger than set1: fruit detail needs resolution)
  min_bbox_area_ratio: 0.006
  reject_truncation_px: 3
  classifier_imgsz: 128
  # Conservative fruit acceptance (wrong pickup = -40, miss = 0):
  conf_threshold: 0.75          # calibrated softmax prob for top-1 fruit
  margin_threshold: 0.15        # top1 - top2 probability
  unknown_conf_relax: 0.50      # below this top-1 conf, treat as unknown regardless of class
  # Tracking + voting:
  track_iou: 0.3
  track_max_age: 12
  vote_window: 9
  min_confirmations: 3          # target-fruit votes needed for TARGET_CONFIRMED
  max_reobserve: 3              # active viewpoint changes for an unknown/uncertain cube
  unknown_patience: 4           # consecutive unknowns before requesting a viewpoint change
  # PICKUP_READY gating:
  pickup_min_bbox_px: 110       # cube must be close (large) to pick
  reconfirm_within: 3           # re-seen as target within N recent frames
  conflict_reject: 2            # >=N strong non-target-fruit votes -> REJECT (never pick)

lines: -  conf_threshold: 0.75          # calibrated softmax prob for top-1 fruit
-  unknown_conf_relax: 0.50      # below this top-1 conf, treat as unknown regardless of class
+  # Conservative fruit acceptance (wrong pickup = -40, miss = 0). Tuned from a val sweep:
+  # at conf 0.90 the per-frame unknown-leak is ~2% (vs 8.6% at 0.75) and, combined with the
+  # 3-frame confirmation + conflict-reject below, the effective false-pickup rate ~ 0.
+  # margin barely binds (softmax is peaky) so it's just a cheap flat-distribution guard.
+  conf_threshold: 0.90          # calibrated softmax prob for top-1 fruit
+  unknown_conf_relax: 0.60      # below this top-1 conf, treat as unknown regardless of class

toolUseResult.originalFile:
"""Stage-2 fruit classifier for Set 2.

Classifies a detector crop into apple / orange / banana / pineapple / unknown.
MobileNetV3-Small backbone (fast on Orin Nano). Emphasis on:
  * conservative behaviour -> a large, well-represented 'unknown' class + temperature
    calibration so the runtime can threshold a *trustworthy* confidence (wrong pickup
    = -40, miss = 0, so we optimize for low false-target rate, not max accuracy)
  * an explicit report of the metric that maps to the penalty: 'unknown leakage'
    (true-unknown crops accepted as a fruit) and fruit<->fruit confusion AT the
    runtime confidence threshold.

Reads ImageFolder data from datasets/set2/classifier/{train,val}/<class>/.
Run in the yolo/ venv:
    yolo/bin/python training/train_set2_classifier.py --epochs 70 --imgsz 128
Output: models/set2/classifier/{best.pt, classes.json, temperature.json, confusion.txt}
"""

import argparse
import json
import os

import numpy as np
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F
from torch.utils.data import DataLoader, WeightedRandomSampler
from torchvision import datasets, transforms, models

ROOT = os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__)))
FRUITS = {"apple", "orange", "banana", "pineapple"}
# Runtime gate this report is evaluated at (keep in sync with configs/set2.yaml runtime).
EVAL_CONF, EVAL_MARGIN = 0.75, 0.15


def build_loaders(data_root, imgsz, batch):
    # Augmentation simulates detector crop imperfection + printed-label / sim->real gap.
    train_tf = transforms.Compose([
        transforms.RandomResizedCrop(imgsz, scale=(0.7, 1.0), ratio=(0.8, 1.25)),
        transforms.RandomApply([transforms.GaussianBlur(3, (0.1, 2.0))], p=0.3),
        transforms.ColorJitter(0.3, 0.3, 0.3, 0.05),
        transforms.RandomRotation(12),
        transforms.RandomPerspective(0.2, p=0.3),         # off-axis printed-label views
        transforms.ToTensor(),
        transforms.Normalize([0.485, 0.456, 0.406], [0.229, 0.224, 0.225]),
        transforms.RandomErasing(p=0.25, scale=(0.02, 0.15)),
    ])
    val_tf = transforms.Compose([
        transforms.Resize((imgsz, imgsz)),
        transforms.ToTensor(),
        transforms.Normalize([0.485, 0.456, 0.406], [0.229, 0.224, 0.225]),
    ])
    train = datasets.ImageFolder(os.path.join(data_root, "train"), train_tf)
    val = datasets.ImageFolder(os.path.join(data_root, "val"), val_tf)

    counts = np.bincount([y for _, y in train.samples], minlength=len(train.classes))
    w = 1.0 / np.maximum(counts, 1)
    sample_w = [w[y] for _, y in train.samples]
    sampler = WeightedRandomSampler(sample_w, len(sample_w), replacement=True)
    return (DataLoader(train, batch_size=batch, sampler=sampler, num_workers=8, pin_memory=True),
            DataLoader(val, batch_size=batch, shuffle=False, num_workers=8, pin_memory=True),
            train.classes, counts)


@torch.no_grad()
def collect_logits(model, loader, device):
    model.eval()
    logits, labels = [], []
    for x, y in loader:
        logits.append(model(x.to(device)).cpu())
        labels.append(y)
    return torch.cat(logits), torch.cat(labels)


def fit_temperature(logits, labels):
    """Temperature scaling: one scalar T minimizing val NLL for calibrated confidence."""
    T = torch.nn.Parameter(torch.ones(1))
    opt = torch.optim.LBFGS([T], lr=0.05, max_iter=60)

    def closure():
        opt.zero_grad()
        loss = F.cross_entropy(logits / T.clamp(min=0.05), labels)
        loss.backward()
        return loss
    opt.step(closure)
    return float(T.detach().clamp(min=0.05))


def report(logits, labels, classes, T, out_dir):
    """Confusion matrix + the penalty-relevant metrics at the runtime gate."""
    z = (logits / T)
    p = torch.softmax(z, 1).numpy()
    pred = p.argmax(1)
    y = labels.numpy()
    n = len(classes)
    cm = np.zeros((n, n), int)
    for t, pr in zip(y, pred):
        cm[t, pr] += 1
    lines = ["confusion matrix (rows=true, cols=pred)", "\t" + "\t".join(classes)]
    for i, c in enumerate(classes):
        lines.append(c + "\t" + "\t".join(str(v) for v in cm[i]))
    for i, c in enumerate(classes):
        tp = cm[i, i]; prec = tp / max(cm[:, i].sum(), 1); rec = tp / max(cm[i].sum(), 1)
        lines.append(f"{c:10s} precision={prec:.3f} recall={rec:.3f}")

    # --- penalty-relevant: behaviour AT the runtime conf+margin gate ---
    order = np.argsort(-p, axis=1)
    top1 = order[:, 0]; top2 = order[:, 1]
    conf = p[np.arange(len(p)), top1]
    margin = conf - p[np.arange(len(p)), top2]
    accepted = (conf >= EVAL_CONF) & (margin >= EVAL_MARGIN)          # crop would be trusted
    is_fruit_pred = np.array([classes[i] in FRUITS for i in top1])
    unk_idx = classes.index("unknown") if "unknown" in classes else -1

    # False target: a NON-matching crop accepted as some fruit. Worst case = true 'unknown'
    # (white face / junk) accepted as a fruit -> would drive a -40 wrong pickup.
    true_unknown = (y == unk_idx)
    leak = accepted & is_fruit_pred & true_unknown
    leak_rate = leak.sum() / max(true_unknown.sum(), 1)
    # Fruit<->fruit confusion among accepted fruit crops (wrong fruit accepted).
    true_fruit = np.array([classes[t] in FRUITS for t in y])
    acc_fruit = accepted & is_fruit_pred & true_fruit
    wrong_fruit = acc_fruit & (top1 != y)
    fruit_conf_rate = wrong_fruit.sum() / max(acc_fruit.sum(), 1)
    # Recall of identifiable fruit (true fruit crops that get accepted as the right fruit).
    fruit_recall = (acc_fruit & (top1 == y)).sum() / max(true_fruit.sum(), 1)

    lines += [
        f"--- at runtime gate conf>={EVAL_CONF} margin>={EVAL_MARGIN} ---",
        f"unknown_leak_rate (junk accepted as fruit) = {leak.sum()}/{true_unknown.sum()} "
        f"= {leak_rate:.4f}   <-- drives -40 penalties, push toward 0",
        f"fruit_confusion_rate (wrong fruit accepted) = {wrong_fruit.sum()}/{acc_fruit.sum()} "
        f"= {fruit_conf_rate:.4f}",
        f"identifiable_fruit_recall = {fruit_recall:.4f}",
    ]
    rep = "\n".join(lines)
    print(rep)
    with open(os.path.join(out_dir, "confusion.txt"), "w") as f:
        f.write(rep + "\n")


def main():
    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser()
    ap.add_argument("--data", default=os.path.join(ROOT, "datasets", "set2", "classifier"))
    ap.add_argument("--epochs", type=int, default=70)
    ap.add_argument("--batch", type=int, default=128)
    ap.add_argument("--imgsz", type=int, default=128)
    ap.add_argument("--lr", type=float, default=3e-4)
    args = ap.parse_args()
    device = "cuda" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu"

    train_loader, val_loader, classes, counts = build_loaders(args.data, args.imgsz, args.batch)
    print("classes:", dict(zip(classes, counts.tolist())))

    model = models.mobilenet_v3_small(weights=models.MobileNet_V3_Small_Weights.DEFAULT)
    model.classifier[3] = nn.Linear(model.classifier[3].in_features, len(classes))
    model = model.to(device)

    crit = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.05)
    opt = torch.optim.AdamW(model.parameters(), lr=args.lr, weight_decay=1e-4)
    sched = torch.optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR(opt, args.epochs)

    out_dir = os.path.join(ROOT, "models", "set2", "classifier")
    os.makedirs(out_dir, exist_ok=True)
    best_acc = 0.0
    for ep in range(args.epochs):
        model.train()
        for x, y in train_loader:
            x, y = x.to(device), y.to(device)
            opt.zero_grad()
            loss = crit(model(x), y)
            loss.backward()
            opt.step()
        sched.step()
        logits, labels = collect_logits(model, val_loader, device)
        acc = (logits.argmax(1) == labels).float().mean().item()
        print(f"epoch {ep + 1}/{args.epochs}  val_acc={acc:.4f}")
        if acc >= best_acc:
            best_acc = acc
            torch.save(model.state_dict(), os.path.join(out_dir, "best.pt"))

    model.load_state_dict(torch.load(os.path.join(out_dir, "best.pt"), map_location=device))
    logits, labels = collect_logits(model, val_loader, device)
    T = fit_temperature(logits, labels)
    print(f"best val_acc={best_acc:.4f}  temperature={T:.3f}")
    report(logits, labels, classes, T, out_dir)

    json.dump(classes, open(os.path.join(out_dir, "classes.json"), "w"))
    json.dump({"temperature": T, "imgsz": args.imgsz,
               "mean": [0.485, 0.456, 0.406], "std": [0.229, 0.224, 0.225]},
              open(os.path.join(out_dir, "temperature.json"), "w"))
    print("saved ->", out_dir)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

aiTitle: Set 2 cube detection and fruit classification pipeline

content.text:
완료했습니다. 분류기는 이대로 **대회 사용 가능** 수준입니다.

## 적용한 것
- `runtime.conf_threshold: 0.75 → 0.90`, `margin_threshold → 0.10`, `unknown_conf_relax → 0.60` ([set2.yaml](configs/set2.yaml))
- 분류기 리포트 동작점도 0.90/0.10로 동기화 ([train_set2_classifier.py](training/train_set2_classifier.py))
- 재학습은 **불필요** (런타임 값만 바꾸면 됨)

## 선택지 (원하면): recall을 더 끌어올리고 싶을 때
지금은 conf 0.90에서 프레임 recall 40%인데, 이게 부담되면 **분류기를 해상도만 높여 재학습**하면 누출↓·recall↑ 둘 다 개선됩니다(과일 디테일이 또렷해짐). Jetson 비용은 거의 안 늘어납니다.
```bash
yolo/bin/python training/train_set2_classifier.py --epochs 70 --imgsz 160
```
(이 경우 `set2.yaml`의 `runtime.classifier_imgsz`도 160으로 맞추세요. 안 해도 대회엔 충분합니다.)

## 다음 할 일
1. **디텍터 학습 완료 대기** → 끝나면 로그의 `recall / mAP50` 붙여주세요.
2. 둘 다 준비되면 **엔드투엔드 점검**:
   ```bash
   yolo/bin/python deployment/run_perception.py --set set2 --target banana --source 0 --show --log
   ```
   - 또렷한 타깃이 `TARGET_CONFIRMED → PICKUP_READY`로 가는지
   - 흰 면/비타깃이 `UNKNOWN_CUBE`/`REJECTED`로 안전하게 빠지는지
3. 그 다음 ONNX/TensorRT 내보내기 → Jetson 배포.

디텍터 결과 나오면 이어서 봐드리겠습니다.

디텍터 아주 잘 나왔습니다 — recall 0.972 / precision 0.992 / mAP50 0.985. 고-recall 우선 목표에 딱 맞고, 작은·먼·벽쪽 큐브도 거의 안 놓친다는 뜻입니다.

⚠️ 단, 이건 **합성 검증셋** 수치라 실제 카메라에선 다소 떨어집니다(sim2real 갭). 그 갭은 보수적 분류기+정책이 흡수하고, 마지막에 실데이터 미세조정으로 메웁니다.

두 모델 다 준비됐으니 이제 **점검 → 배포** 단계입니다.

## 1) 엔드투엔드 점검 (웹캠/영상으로, 선택)
```bash
yolo/bin/python deployment/run_perception.py --set set2 --target banana --source 0 --show --log
```
- 또렷한 타깃: `TARGET_CONFIRMED → PICKUP_READY`로 가는지
- 흰 면/비타깃: `UNKNOWN_CUBE`/`REJECTED`로 안전하게 빠지는지
- `--log`로 애매한 크롭이 `runtime_logs/set2/`에 쌓이는지 (나중에 미세조정 재료)

## 2) ONNX 내보내기 (개발 PC, WSL)
```bash
yolo/bin/python deployment/export_set2_onnx.py
```
→ `models/set2/detector/best.onnx`, `models/set2/classifier/best.onnx`

## 3) Jetson Orin Nano에서 TensorRT 엔진 빌드 + 실행
엔진은 기기 종속이라 **반드시 Jetson 위에서** 빌드합니다.
```bash
python deployment/build_set2_tensorrt.py --half          # FP16 권장
python deployment/run_perception.py --set set2 --target <fruit> --left 0 --right 1 --show
```
`run_perception.py`는 `.engine`이 있으면 자동으로 사용합니다.

## 4) (대회 전) 실데이터 미세조정
실제 두 카메라로 각 과일·**흰 면만 보이는 큐브**·애매 크롭을 찍어 `unknown` 위주로 라벨 → 두 모델 fine-tune. 배포 중 `--log`로 모은 실패 크롭도 재료로 넣습니다.

---

지금 바로는 **2) ONNX 내보내기**를 돌려보시는 걸 권합니다. 제가 WSL에서 대신 실행해 드릴까요? (export는 GPU/시간 부담이 적습니다.) 아니면 점검(1번)부터 보시겠어요?
좋아 지금 export set2 onnx 실행했고, 지금 teamtwo@102.168.0.1 기준으로 ssh로 복사, 엔진 빌드까지 과정을 다시 보여줘

먼저 짚을 게 있습니다: **IP가 `102.168.0.1`로 적혀 있는데 사설망 주소가 아닙니다.** `192.168.0.1`(흔한 오타)일 가능성이 높으니 실제 Jetson IP를 확인해 주세요. 아래는 `192.168.0.1` 기준으로 적었습니다.

그리고 코드를 점검하다 보니 **런타임이 디텍터를 항상 `best.pt`(PyTorch)로 로드**하게 돼 있어, 엔진을 빌드해도 안 쓰입니다. 엔진을 자동으로 쓰도록 먼저 고치겠습니다.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
"""Set 2 runtime perception: detector -> crop -> conservative fruit classifier
-> per-camera track -> vote -> Set2DecisionPolicy.

Loads ONLY the Set 2 models (set2_cube_detector + set2_fruit_classifier). The
detector (ultralytics .pt/.onnx/.engine) finds cube candidates at high recall; each
crop is classified by MobileNetV3 (torch .pt or .onnx) into apple/orange/banana/
pineapple/unknown with temperature-calibrated confidence; results feed an IoU tracker
and the conservative decision policy.

Dual cameras: call process_frame(frame, camera='left'|'right'). A separate tracker per
camera keeps IoU association valid (the two views don't share image coordinates); a
cube that is 'unknown' in one camera can be classified by the other. Cross-camera
fusion of the SAME physical cube needs a world-position estimate (LiDAR/odometry) and
is left to the navigation layer; pass world_pos to process_frame to enable proximity
fusion if you have it.
"""

import json
import os

import numpy as np

from runtime.tracking import Tracker
from runtime.set2_decision_policy import Set2DecisionPolicy

ROOT = os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__)))


def build_classifier_torch(num_classes):
    import torch.nn as nn
    from torchvision import models
    m = models.mobilenet_v3_small(weights=None)
    m.classifier[3] = nn.Linear(m.classifier[3].in_features, num_classes)
    return m


class FruitClassifier:
    """Loads a MobileNetV3 fruit classifier (.pt or .onnx) + calibration metadata."""

    def __init__(self, model_dir):
        self.classes = json.load(open(os.path.join(model_dir, "classes.json")))
        meta = json.load(open(os.path.join(model_dir, "temperature.json")))
        self.T = meta["temperature"]
        self.imgsz = meta["imgsz"]
        self.mean = np.array(meta["mean"], np.float32)
        self.std = np.array(meta["std"], np.float32)

        pt, onnx = os.path.join(model_dir, "best.pt"), os.path.join(model_dir, "best.onnx")
        if os.path.isfile(onnx):
            import onnxruntime as ort
            self.backend = "onnx"
            self.sess = ort.InferenceSession(onnx, providers=[
                "TensorrtExecutionProvider", "CUDAExecutionProvider", "CPUExecutionProvider"])
            self.inp = self.sess.get_inputs()[0].name
        else:
            import torch
            self.backend = "torch"
            self.torch = torch
            self.device = "cuda" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu"
            self.model = build_classifier_torch(len(self.classes)).to(self.device).eval()
            self.model.load_state_dict(torch.load(pt, map_location=self.device))

    def _pre(self, crop_rgb):
        import cv2
        img = cv2.resize(crop_rgb, (self.imgsz, self.imgsz)).astype(np.float32) / 255.0
        img = (img - self.mean) / self.std
        return np.transpose(img, (2, 0, 1))[None].astype(np.float32)

    def predict(self, crop_rgb):
        x = self._pre(crop_rgb)
        if self.backend == "onnx":
            logits = self.sess.run(None, {self.inp: x})[0][0]
        else:
            with self.torch.no_grad():
                logits = self.model(self.torch.from_numpy(x).to(self.device)).cpu().numpy()[0]
        z = logits / self.T
        e = np.exp(z - z.max()); p = e / e.sum()
        order = p.argsort()[::-1]
        top1, top2 = order[0], order[1]
        return self.classes[top1], float(p[top1]), float(p[top1] - p[top2])


class Set2Pipeline:
    def __init__(self, cfg, target_fruit):
        from ultralytics import YOLO
        rt = cfg["runtime"]
        self.rt = rt
        self.target = target_fruit
        self.detector = YOLO(os.path.join(ROOT, "models", "set2", "detector", "best.pt"))
        self.clf = FruitClassifier(os.path.join(ROOT, "models", "set2", "classifier"))
        self.trackers = {}                         # camera -> Tracker (per-view association)
        self.policy = Set2DecisionPolicy(rt, target_fruit)
        self.frame_idx = 0

    def _tracker(self, camera):
        if camera not in self.trackers:
            self.trackers[camera] = Tracker(self.rt["track_iou"], self.rt["track_max_age"],
                                            self.rt["vote_window"])
        return self.trackers[camera]

    def _classifiable(self, box, W, H):
        """Only classify a cube that is close/large/untruncated enough to read fruit."""
        x0, y0, x1, y1 = box
        if min(x1 - x0, y1 - y0) < self.rt["min_bbox_px"]:
            return False
        if (x1 - x0) * (y1 - y0) / (W * H) < self.rt["min_bbox_area_ratio"]:
            return False
        m = self.rt["reject_truncation_px"]
        if x0 <= m or y0 <= m or x1 >= W - m or y1 >= H - m:
            return False
        return True

    def note_reobserved(self, camera, track_id):
        """Navigator calls this after it has moved the robot to honour a re-observe request."""
        tracker = self.trackers.get(camera)
        if tracker is None:
            return
        for t in tracker.tracks:
            if t.id == track_id:
                t.reobserve_count = getattr(t, "reobserve_count", 0) + 1

    def process_frame(self, frame_bgr, camera="left", world_pos=None):
        """Run one frame from one camera. Returns list of per-detection result dicts."""
        import cv2
        H, W = frame_bgr.shape[:2]
        rgb = cv2.cvtColor(frame_bgr, cv2.COLOR_BGR2RGB)
        res = self.detector.predict(frame_bgr, conf=self.rt["detector_conf"],
                                    imgsz=self.rt["detector_imgsz"], verbose=False)[0]
        boxes = [tuple(map(float, b)) for b in res.boxes.xyxy.cpu().numpy()] if res.boxes else []

        tracker = self._tracker(camera)
        matched = tracker.update(boxes, self.frame_idx)
        out = []
        for track, box in matched:
            cls, conf, margin = None, 0.0, 0.0
            if self._classifiable(box, W, H):
                x0, y0, x1, y1 = (int(max(0, box[0])), int(max(0, box[1])),
                                  int(min(W, box[2])), int(min(H, box[3])))
                crop = rgb[y0:y1, x0:x1]
                if crop.size:
                    cls, conf, margin = self.clf.predict(crop)
                    track.add_obs(cls, conf, margin, self.frame_idx)
            state, info = self.policy.evaluate(track)
            info["camera"] = camera
            out.append({"bbox": box, "state": state, "cls": cls, "conf": conf,
                        "margin": margin, "camera": camera, "track": track.id,
                        "request_reobserve": info["request_reobserve"], "info": info})
        self.frame_idx += 1
        return out

content.text:
수정 반영했습니다 (`runtime/set2_pipeline.py`: 디텍터를 `engine → onnx → pt` 순으로 자동 선택). 이제 복사부터 빌드·실행까지 전체 절차입니다.

## 0) 사전 확인
```bash
# Jetson IP 확인 (102.x.x.x는 사설망이 아님 → 십중팔구 192.168.0.1 오타)
ping -c1 192.168.0.1
```

## 1) WSL → Jetson 복사 (venv·데이터·런 결과는 제외)
Jetson은 자체 JetPack 파이썬을 쓰므로 무거운 `yolo/`, `datasets/`, `runs/`는 빼고 보냅니다.
```bash
rsync -avz \
  --exclude 'yolo/' --exclude 'datasets/' --exclude 'runs/' \
  --exclude '.git/' --exclude '__pycache__/' --exclude '*.pyc' \
  /home/user/joon/  teamtwo@192.168.0.1:~/joon/
```
rsync가 없으면 scp로:
```bash
scp -r models configs runtime deployment sim assets teamtwo@192.168.0.1:~/joon/
```
(빌드엔 `models/set2/` + `configs/` + `runtime/` + `deployment/`면 충분합니다. 미세조정/디버그를 위해 `sim`,`assets`도 같이 보내면 편합니다.)

## 2) Jetson 접속 + 의존성 확인
```bash
ssh teamtwo@192.168.0.1
cd ~/joon
python3 -c "import ultralytics, cv2; print('ultralytics ok')"
python3 -c "import onnxruntime as ort; print(ort.get_available_providers())"   # TensorrtExecutionProvider 보이면 best
ls /usr/src/tensorrt/bin/trtexec      # 분류기 엔진 빌드에 필요
```

## 3) TensorRT 엔진 빌드 (반드시 Jetson 위에서)
```bash
python3 deployment/build_set2_tensorrt.py --half
```
- **디텍터**: `best.pt → best.engine` (Ultralytics, NMS 포함) → 런타임이 자동 사용.
- **분류기**: `best.onnx → best.engine` (trtexec). 실제 런타임 가속은 onnxruntime의 **TensorRT EP가 `best.onnx`에서** 처리하므로, 이 단계가 실패해도(예: trtexec 경로 문제) 디텍터 엔진만으로 주된 속도 이득은 확보됩니다. onnxruntime가 없으면 분류기는 `best.pt`로 자동 폴백합니다.

## 4) 실행
```bash
ls /dev/video*                      # 카메라 인덱스 확인 (예: video0, video1)
python3 deployment/run_perception.py --set set2 --target banana --left 0 --right 1 --show
```
시작 로그에 `[set2] detector backend: best.engine` 가 찍히면 엔진을 쓰는 것입니다.

---

확인 포인트
- 첫 실행 시 onnxruntime-TRT는 엔진을 캐시하느라 **처음 몇 초 느릴 수 있음** (이후 정상).
- 또렷한 타깃 → `PICKUP_READY`, 흰 면/비타깃 → `UNKNOWN_CUBE`/`REJECTED` 로 가는지.
- FPS/지연이 궁금하면 알려주세요 — 보통 디텍터가 지연을 좌우하고, FP16 엔진으로 Orin Nano에서 실시간 나옵니다.

실행 로그나 FPS 붙여주시면 다음 단계(임계값 미세조정·실데이터 수집 루프) 이어가겠습니다.
